evidence_quotes.quote: Currently video game developers that sell online games are able to shut down servers that these games need to run and make the games impossible to play.

paragraphs.text: Hello, There is an issue that I believe should be covered under the Digital Fairness Act. Currently video game developers that sell online games are able to shut down servers that these games need to run and make the games impossible to play. Video game developers such as Capcom, EA, Microsoft Studios, Nintendo, Sony and many more (please see unofficial list of dead / at-risk games on the stopkillinggames wiki) have sold online only games and have no end of life plan for when they are shut down. Creating an end of life plan will not come at a large expense to the game developers. An end of life plan would allow the games to be played on offline mode or on fan servers with no continued cost to the developers after initial set-up. I believe that allowing companies to destroy online video games is being exploited. Companies that do not want to compete with older games that they created when they release a new version. For example if EA wanted to release The Sims 5, they can destroy The Sims 4 and force Sims fans to buy the new version. Thank you for reading and I hope that you consider my concerns.
